words.definitionEn: a thing that may happen or be true; the fact that something might happen or be true, but is not certain
words.exampleEn: Bankruptcy is a real possibility if sales don't improve.

words.definitionEn: a large notice, often with a picture on it, that is put in a public place to advertise something
words.exampleEn: campaign/election posters

words.definitionEn: the ability to control people or things
words.exampleEn: power to do something, He has the power to make things very unpleasant for us.

words.definitionEn: to say that something will happen in the future
words.exampleEn: predict something, a reliable method of predicting earthquakes

words.definitionEn: as stated or reported by somebody/something
words.exampleEn: According to Mick, it's a great movie.

words.definitionEn: that you can get, buy or find
words.exampleEn: available resources/data

words.definitionEn: to show or offer something for other people to look at or consider
words.exampleEn: present something, Are you presenting a paper at the conference?

words.definitionEn: calculated by adding several amounts together, finding a total, and dividing the total by the number of amounts
words.exampleEn: The average age of participants was 52 years.

words.definitionEn: the leader of a republic, for example the US
words.exampleEn: Several presidents attended the funeral.

words.definitionEn: the result of adding several amounts together, finding a total, and dividing the total by the number of amounts
words.exampleEn: The average of 4, 5 and 9 is 6.

words.definitionEn: to stop somebody from doing something; to stop something from happening
words.exampleEn: prevent something/somebody, The accident could have been prevented.

words.definitionEn: to prevent something bad from happening
words.exampleEn: avoid something, to avoid conflict/confrontation

words.definitionEn: to produce letters, pictures, etc. on paper using a machine that puts ink (= coloured liquid) on the surface
words.exampleEn: print (something), I'm printing a copy of the document for you.

words.definitionEn: a machine for printing text on paper, especially one connected to a computer
words.exampleEn: a colour printer

words.definitionEn: a building where people are kept as a punishment for a crime they have committed, or while they are waiting for trial
words.exampleEn: She went to prison for tax evasion.

words.definitionEn: an award that is given to a person who wins a competition, race, etc. or who does very good work
words.exampleEn: She was awarded the Nobel Peace Prize.

words.definitionEn: a prize such as money, etc. for something that somebody has done
words.exampleEn: He was nominated for the best actor award.

words.definitionEn: a series of things that are done in order to achieve a particular result
words.exampleEn: the consultation/planning process

words.definitionEn: to make things to be sold, especially in large quantities
words.exampleEn: Our company mainly produces goods for export.

words.definitionEn: doing something as a paid job rather than as a hobby
words.exampleEn: professional athletes/players

words.definitionEn: a university teacher of the highest rank
words.exampleEn: Professor (Ann) Williams

words.definitionEn: a description of somebody/something that gives useful information
words.exampleEn: We first build up a detailed profile of our customers and their requirements.

words.definitionEn: a set of instructions in code that control the operations or functions of a computer
words.exampleEn: You'll need to download and install the program on your computer.

words.definitionEn: the process of improving or developing, or of getting nearer to achieving or completing something
words.exampleEn: I think we're making progress.

words.definitionEn: a statement that tells somebody that you will definitely do or not do something
words.exampleEn: to make/keep/fulfil/break a promise

words.definitionEn: to tell somebody that you will definitely do or not do something, or that something will definitely happen
words.exampleEn: ‘We haven't got time to go to the park.’ ‘But you promised!’

words.definitionEn: to make the sound of a word or letter in a particular way
words.exampleEn: to pronounce a word/syllable/vowel/consonant

words.definitionEn: very bad or unpleasant
words.exampleEn: That's an awful colour.

words.definitionEn: to make sure that somebody/something is not harmed, injured, damaged, etc.
words.exampleEn: protect somebody/something/yourself, to protect children/citizens/civilians/consumers/workers

words.definitionEn: to give something to somebody or make it available for them to use
words.exampleEn: provide something, Please provide the following information.

words.definitionEn: located behind or at the back of something
words.exampleEn: We were sitting in the back row

words.definitionEn: a building where people go to drink and meet their friends. Pubs serve alcoholic and other drinks, and often also food.
words.exampleEn: We're all going to the pub after work.

words.definitionEn: connected with ordinary people in society in general
words.exampleEn: The campaign is designed to increase public awareness of the issues.

words.definitionEn: ordinary people in society in general
words.exampleEn: The palace is now open to the public.

words.definitionEn: to produce a book, magazine, CD-ROM, etc. and sell it to the public
words.exampleEn: to publish a book/novel/magazine/paper

words.definitionEn: to take hold of something and use force in order to move it or try to move it towards yourself
words.exampleEn: You push and I'll pull.

words.definitionEn: the intention, aim or function of something; the thing that something is supposed to achieve
words.exampleEn: Our campaign's main purpose is to raise money.

words.definitionEn: to use your hands, arms or body in order to make somebody/something move forward or away from you; to move part of your body into a particular position
words.exampleEn: We pushed and pushed but the piano wouldn't move.

words.definitionEn: the standard of something when it is compared to other things like it; how good or bad something is
words.exampleEn: Improving the quality of care for nursing home residents is a priority.

words.definitionEn: an amount or a number of something
words.exampleEn: quantity of something, a large/small quantity of something

words.definitionEn: the female ruler of an independent state that has a royal family
words.exampleEn: She was crowned queen at the age of fifteen.

words.definitionEn: to ask somebody questions about something, especially officially
words.exampleEn: question somebody, Police are keen to question any witnesses.

words.definitionEn: the details of a person’s family, education, experience, etc.
words.exampleEn: somebody's ethnic/genetic/cultural background

words.definitionEn: in a way that makes very little noise
words.exampleEn: to ask/speak/talk quietly

words.definitionEn: a competition between people, animals, vehicles, etc. to see which one is the faster or fastest
words.exampleEn: a boat/horse race

words.definitionEn: to compete against somebody/something to see who can go faster or the fastest, do something first, etc.; to take part in a race or races
words.exampleEn: They raced to a thrilling victory in the relay.

words.definitionEn: a track with rails on which trains run
words.exampleEn: The railway is still under construction.

words.definitionEn: to lift or move something to a higher level
words.exampleEn: She raised the gun and fired.

words.definitionEn: a measurement of the speed at which something happens
words.exampleEn: rate (of something), Figures published today show another fall in the rate of inflation.

words.definitionEn: used to mean ‘fairly’ or ‘to some degree’, often when you are disappointed, surprised or expressing slight criticism
words.exampleEn: rather odd/strange/unusual

words.definitionEn: to arrive at the place that you have been travelling to
words.exampleEn: They didn't reach the border until after dark.

words.definitionEn: to change or behave in a particular way as a result of or in response to something
words.exampleEn: I nudged her but she didn't react.

words.definitionEn: to understand or become aware of a particular fact or situation
words.exampleEn: realize (that)…, I didn’t realize (that) you were so unhappy.

words.definitionEn: without skill or care
words.exampleEn: to play/sing badly

words.definitionEn: to get or accept something that is sent or given to you
words.exampleEn: receive something

words.definitionEn: that happened or began only a short time ago
words.exampleEn: a recent study/report/survey

words.definitionEn: not long ago
words.exampleEn: a recently published book

words.definitionEn: the area inside the entrance of a hotel, an office building, etc. where guests or visitors go first when they arrive
words.exampleEn: Please report to reception on arrival.

words.definitionEn: a set of instructions that tells you how to cook something and the ingredients (= items of food) you need for it
words.exampleEn: recipe for something, a recipe for chicken soup

words.definitionEn: to know who somebody is or what something is when you see or hear them or it, because you have seen or heard them or it before
words.exampleEn: recognize somebody/something, I recognized him as soon as he came in the room.

words.definitionEn: to tell somebody that something is good or useful, or that somebody would be suitable for a particular job, etc.
words.exampleEn: recommend somebody/something, The hotel's new restaurant comes highly recommended (= a lot of people have praised it).

words.definitionEn: a written account of something that is kept so that it can be looked at and used in the future
words.exampleEn: You should keep a record of your expenses.

words.definitionEn: to keep a permanent account of facts or events by writing them down, filming them, storing them in a computer, etc.
words.exampleEn: record something, Her childhood is recorded in the diaries of those years.

words.definitionEn: sound or pictures that have been recorded on computer files or on CD, DVD, video, etc.
words.exampleEn: an audio recording

words.definitionEn: to treat things that have already been used so that they can be used again
words.exampleEn: to recycle waste/rubbish

words.definitionEn: to make something less or smaller in size, quantity, price, etc.; to become less or smaller in size, quantity, etc.
words.exampleEn: reduce something, Reduce speed now (= on a sign).

words.definitionEn: to send somebody/something to somebody/something for help, advice or a decision
words.exampleEn: refer somebody/something to somebody/something, My doctor referred me to a specialist.

words.definitionEn: to say or show that you will not do something that somebody has asked you to do
words.exampleEn: Go on, ask her; she can hardly refuse.

words.definitionEn: a large area of land, usually without exact limits or borders
words.exampleEn: mountainous/coastal regions

words.definitionEn: following a pattern, especially with the same time or space in between each thing and the next
words.exampleEn: regular breathing

words.definitionEn: the way in which two people, groups or countries behave towards each other or deal with each other
words.exampleEn: a personal/working relationship

words.definitionEn: to take somebody/something away from a place
words.exampleEn: remove somebody/something, Illegally parked vehicles will be removed.

words.definitionEn: to fix something that is broken, damaged or torn
words.exampleEn: to repair a car/television

words.definitionEn: to be used instead of somebody/something else; to do something instead of somebody/something else
words.exampleEn: The new design will eventually replace all existing models.

words.definitionEn: an act of replying to something/somebody in speech, writing or by some action
words.exampleEn: I asked her what her name was but she made no reply.

words.definitionEn: to say or write something as an answer to somebody/something
words.exampleEn: She didn't even bother to reply.

words.definitionEn: to give people information about something that you have heard, seen, done, etc.
words.exampleEn: report something, The crash happened seconds after the pilot reported engine trouble.

words.definitionEn: a person who collects and reports news for newspapers, radio or television
words.exampleEn: He leaked the story to a news reporter.

words.definitionEn: the action of asking for something formally and politely; a thing that you formally ask for
words.exampleEn: request for something, a request for information

words.definitionEn: a careful study of a subject, especially in order to discover new facts or information about it
words.exampleEn: scientific/medical/academic research

words.definitionEn: to study something carefully and try to discover new facts about it
words.exampleEn: research (something), to research a topic/subject

words.definitionEn: a person who studies something carefully and tries to discover new facts about it
words.exampleEn: European researchers say olive oil could help prevent cancer.

words.definitionEn: to give a spoken or written answer to somebody/something
words.exampleEn: I asked him his name, but he didn't respond.

words.definitionEn: a place where you can buy and drink alcoholic and other drinks
words.exampleEn: We arranged to meet in a bar called the Flamingo.

words.definitionEn: a spoken or written answer
words.exampleEn: She made no response.

words.definitionEn: the part of something that remains
words.exampleEn: Take what you want and throw the rest away.

words.definitionEn: to relax, sleep or do nothing after a period of activity or illness; to not use a part of your body for some time
words.exampleEn: The doctor told me to rest.

words.definitionEn: a report in a newspaper or magazine, or on the internet, television or radio, in which somebody gives their opinion of a book, play, film, product, etc.; the act of writing this kind of report
words.exampleEn: a book review

words.definitionEn: to write a report of a book, play, film, product, etc. in which you give your opinion of it
words.exampleEn: Please rate and review your purchase on our website.

words.definitionEn: a short journey in a vehicle, on a bicycle, etc.
words.exampleEn: a train ride through beautiful countryside

words.definitionEn: a piece of jewellery that you wear on your finger, consisting of a round band of gold, silver, etc., sometimes decorated with precious stones
words.exampleEn: a gold ring

words.definitionEn: to surround somebody/something
words.exampleEn: Thousands of demonstrators ringed the building.

words.definitionEn: to come or go upwards; to reach a higher level or position
words.exampleEn: The curtain rose to reveal an empty stage.

words.definitionEn: the hard solid material that forms part of the surface of the earth and some other planets
words.exampleEn: They drilled through several layers of rock to reach the oil.

words.definitionEn: an actor’s part in a play, film, etc.
words.exampleEn: She has landed the lead role (= the most important one) in a new play.

words.definitionEn: the structure that covers or forms the top of a building or vehicle
words.exampleEn: a flat/sloping/pitched roof

words.definitionEn: having a shape like a circle or a ball
words.exampleEn: a round plate

words.definitionEn: a game played especially in the US by two teams of nine players, using a bat and ball. Each player tries to hit the ball and then run around four bases before the other team can return the ball.
words.exampleEn: a baseball bat/team/cap/fan/player/game

words.definitionEn: moving in a circle
words.exampleEn: Everybody joins hands and dances round.

words.definitionEn: in a circle
words.exampleEn: the first woman to sail round the world

words.definitionEn: a way that you follow to get from one place to another
words.exampleEn: Which is the best route to take?

words.definitionEn: if one thing is based on another, it uses it or is developed from it
words.exampleEn: The movie is based on a real-life incident.

words.definitionEn: things that you throw away because you no longer want or need them
words.exampleEn: a rubbish bag/bin

words.definitionEn: having or showing a lack of respect for other people and their feelings
words.exampleEn: a rude comment/remark

words.definitionEn: an act of running; a period of time spent running or the distance that somebody runs
words.exampleEn: I go for a run every morning.

words.definitionEn: a person or an animal that runs, especially one taking part in a race
words.exampleEn: a long-distance/cross-country/marathon runner

words.definitionEn: the action or sport of running
words.exampleEn: to go running

words.definitionEn: in a sad way
words.exampleEn: ‘I'm so sorry,’ she said sadly.

words.definitionEn: protected from any danger, harm or loss
words.exampleEn: The children are quite safe here.

words.definitionEn: to travel on water using sails or an engine
words.exampleEn: + adv./prep., to sail into harbour

words.definitionEn: the sport or activity of travelling in a boat with sails
words.exampleEn: to go sailing

words.definitionEn: money that employees receive for doing their job, especially professional employees or people working in an office, usually paid every month
words.exampleEn: an annual salary of $40 000

words.definitionEn: an act or the process of selling something
words.exampleEn: regulations governing the sale of alcoholic beverages

words.definitionEn: a thick liquid that is eaten with food to add taste to it
words.exampleEn: tomato/cranberry/chilli sauce

words.definitionEn: to keep somebody/something safe from death, harm, loss, etc.
words.exampleEn: save somebody/something, to save somebody’s life

words.definitionEn: frightened of something or afraid that something bad might happen
words.exampleEn: The thieves got scared and ran away.

words.definitionEn: frightening
words.exampleEn: It was a really scary moment.

words.definitionEn: a game played by two teams of five players, using a large ball which players try to throw into a high net hanging from a ring
words.exampleEn: a basketball game/coach/team/player

words.definitionEn: the place where something happens, especially something unpleasant
words.exampleEn: the scene of the accident/crime/crash

words.definitionEn: a plan that lists all the work that you have to do and when you must do each thing
words.exampleEn: I have a hectic schedule for the next few days.

words.definitionEn: the number of points, goals, etc. scored by each player or team in a game or competition
words.exampleEn: a high/low score

words.definitionEn: to win points, goals, etc. in a game or competition
words.exampleEn: Fraser scored again in the second half.

words.definitionEn: the flat surface at the front of a television, computer, or other electronic device, on which you see pictures or information
words.exampleEn: a computer screen

words.definitionEn: an attempt to find somebody/something, especially by looking carefully for them/it
words.exampleEn: search for somebody/something, a long search for the murder weapon

words.definitionEn: to look carefully for something/somebody; to examine a particular place when looking for something/somebody
words.exampleEn: search for something/somebody, She searched in vain for her passport.

words.definitionEn: any of the four main periods of the year: spring, summer, autumn and winter
words.exampleEn: the changing seasons

words.definitionEn: a place where you can sit, for example a chair
words.exampleEn: She sat back in her seat.

words.definitionEn: after one other person or thing in order or importance
words.exampleEn: She came second in the marathon.

words.definitionEn: used to introduce the second of a list of points you want to make in a speech or piece of writing
words.exampleEn: Firstly, it's expensive, and secondly, it's too slow.

words.definitionEn: known about by only a few people; kept hidden from others
words.exampleEn: secret information/meetings/talks

words.definitionEn: something that is known about by only a few people and not told to others
words.exampleEn: Can you keep a secret?

words.definitionEn: a person who works in an office, working for another person, dealing with mail and phone calls, keeping records, arranging meetings with people, etc.
words.exampleEn: a legal/medical secretary

words.definitionEn: to give the impression of being or doing something
words.exampleEn: + adj., You seem happy.

words.definitionEn: an understanding about something; an ability to judge something
words.exampleEn: One of the most important things in a partner is a sense of humour (= the ability to find things funny or make people laugh).

words.definitionEn: forming a unit by itself; not joined to something else
words.exampleEn: They have begun to sleep in separate rooms.
